content:
# Spoke Dashboard Integration (Phase 2) -- Decision Record (2026-03-28)

## What: Added spoke container visibility to the existing dashboard AgentsView via GET /api/dashboard/spokes endpoint and frontend enrichment.

## Key Design Decisions:
1. Raw Docker Unix socket HTTP instead of bollard crate in corvia-server. Bollard pulls axum 0.7 which breaks all axum 0.8 handler trait bounds when any bollard type appears in the compilation unit. Even type-erased (dyn Any) or tokio::spawn-isolated approaches failed. The only working solution: query Docker directly via HTTP/1.0 over Unix socket with serde deserialization.
2. Removed spoke_count/spokes_running from DashboardStatusResponse. Adding any async call to status_handler pushes its future past axum 0.8's handler trait bound limit (dual axum issue). Frontend computes counts client-side from the dedicated /spokes endpoint.
3. docker_available flag (bool) in AppState instead of cached Docker client. Startup-time check, actual queries created on-demand in tokio::spawn tasks.
4. SpokeInfo extended with created, health, repo_url fields in corvia-kernel for dashboard consumption.

## Review Findings Worth Remembering:
- The corvia.repo_url Docker label was missing from SpokeProvisioner::create(). Without it, issue links in the dashboard are always dead links (#).
- Any reference to corvia_kernel::spoke types from corvia-server (even in separate submodules, even via dyn Any) causes the dual-axum handler trait bound failure. The ONLY safe approach: zero bollard/spoke imports in corvia-server, query Docker directly.
- status_handler is already at the complexity limit for axum 0.8 Handler trait. Adding ANY additional .await call to it (even tokio::spawn) breaks compilation.

## What Blocked / Surprised:
- Dual axum 0.7/0.8 was far more aggressive than expected. Not just handler futures, but ANY type transitively containing bollard types in the same crate breaks ALL handlers. Required 4 iterations to find a working approach (direct bollard -> type-erased dyn Any -> SpokeProvisioner in AppState -> raw Unix socket).
